about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Car rental in Herceg Novi with Igalo, Bijela and airport pickup
about.seoDesc: Herceg Novi rentals made for outer-bay drives: fixed rates, insurance bundled in, Tivat or Dubrovnik airport collection, and local support from drivers who know the Orjen road.
about.p1: Car Rental Herceg Novi connects travellers with reliable rental cars in the outer Bay of Kotor. We work with trusted local operators to offer a range of vehicles, from compact city cars for the stepped lanes of the Old Town to 4x4s for the Orjen road up to Ledenice.
about.heading2: Why a Car Makes Sense in Herceg Novi
about.p2: Public transport around the outer bay is limited: buses follow the magistrala and skip the side roads entirely. The Kamenari ferry shortcut across the Verige strait, the day trip to Rose on Luštica, the border run to Dubrovnik 40 minutes west, and the switchback climb up Orjen all reward travellers with their own wheels. A rental car turns a two-day stop into a proper exploration of the outer bay and the mountains behind it.
about.p3a: We operate car rental services across
about.p3d: , and locations throughout the coast and interior.
about.promise1: Comprehensive cover with zero-excess upgrade available
about.promise2: Tivat or Dubrovnik airport meet-and-greet, your car waits at arrivals
about.promise3: Transparent pricing, the quoted figure is the final figure
about.promise4: Cross-border travel to Croatia, Bosnia, Albania, Serbia, and Kosovo
about.localText: Based in Herceg Novi, we know every stretch of road around the outer bay, the Kamenari ferry timetable, the Debeli Brijeg queue times, the Orjen switchbacks, and the parking tricks along Šetalište Pet Danica that guidebooks never mention. Whether you are heading from
about.localMid2: stepped Old Town, crossing on the Kamenari ferry to
about.localEnd: , or climbing the Orjen road toward Ledenice viewpoint, we can help you map out the ideal route.
about.contactText: Questions about renting a car in Herceg Novi? We are available by phone, WhatsApp, and Viber. Browse the fleet online and reserve in minutes, or drop us a message and we will help you find the right vehicle for your trip.

0.text: We flew into Dubrovnik, collected the car at the airport, and were on the Herceg Novi waterfront within the hour. Spent the first afternoon on the Forte Mare battlements for sunset, the next morning climbing the 100,000 Steps to Savina monastery. The Orjen drive up to Ledenice on day three was the highlight, switchback after switchback, and the outer bay opening up behind us. Booking was painless, paperwork ready, zero fuss., Freya & Magnus, Bergen

1.text: Third trip to Herceg Novi in five years and the booking was the smoothest yet. The Kamenari ferry over to Luštica for a Mamula boat morning, then back across for lunch in Perast, a rental car makes that itinerary easy, otherwise it's two full days. Useful that the team warned us about the Debeli Brijeg queue on Saturday afternoons before we set off for Dubrovnik. Saved us a hot hour sitting at the border., Malcolm Ferriss, Edinburgh

2.text: We based ourselves in Igalo for the spa and used the car for day trips: Dubrovnik one morning, Trebinje over the Bosnian border another, Kotor via the Kamenari ferry on a third. Pickup was outside our apartment rather than the airport, small touch, big help with a toddler. The zero-excess upgrade paid for itself the first time we met oncoming traffic on the Orjen hairpins., Siobhan O'Rourke, Cork

0.q: What do I need to bring for pickup?
0.a: A valid driving licence, your passport or national ID, and a physical credit card in the lead driver's name. Licences without Latin characters also require an International Driving Permit.

1.q: Can I cross into Croatia or other countries?
1.a: Absolutely. The Debeli Brijeg crossing into Croatia is just 35 minutes from Herceg Novi, the quickest international border in the country. Green Card paperwork is included at booking. Bosnia (via Ivanica into the Sutorina valley for Trebinje), Albania, Serbia, and Kosovo are also available with advance notice.

2.q: What age do I need to be?
2.a: The minimum is 21 years old with at least two years of driving experience. Drivers under 25 pay a young-driver supplement, the exact amount depends on the vehicle category.

3.q: What does the rental price cover?
3.a: Every booking includes CDW insurance, third-party liability, VAT, and a roadside safety kit. Most vehicles offer unlimited mileage, check the individual listing for details.

4.q: How do I collect the car at the airport?
4.a: Herceg Novi sits between two airports at roughly equal driving time. A representative meets you inside arrivals at either Tivat (TIV) or Dubrovnik (DBV) with a name board. Your car is parked within a short walk of the terminal. Drive time to the Herceg Novi waterfront is about 40 minutes from either side, Tivat via the Kamenari ferry, Dubrovnik via the Debeli Brijeg crossing.

5.q: What if I need to cancel?
5.a: Most vehicles offer free cancellation up to seven days before pickup. Premium and convertible classes require thirty days. A small processing fee applies to all cancellations.

6.a: Most cars require between €100 and €350 held on a credit card. Some economy vehicles are deposit-free, these are clearly marked in your search results.

7.a: Many vehicles include unlimited mileage. Those with limits charge a per-kilometre rate beyond the allowance, always visible before you confirm.

8.a: None. The displayed price includes every tax and fee. No airport surcharges, no fuel-policy add-ons, nothing extra at the counter.

9.q: How much is the booking deposit?
9.a: A 15-20% advance payment secures your reservation. The balance is settled directly with the rental agent when you arrive to collect the vehicle.

10.q: Can I rent without a credit card?
10.a: A handful of vehicles are available without a credit card requirement. Look for the no-deposit filter when searching by pickup location.

aboutPage.title: About Car Rental Herceg Novi
aboutPage.subtitle: A local booking partner on Montenegro's western border, serving visitors via Tivat Airport, Dubrovnik Airport, or ferry arrivals
aboutPage.description: Car Rental Herceg Novi is an independent booking platform for rental cars on Montenegro's western bay, specialising in Herceg Novi, Igalo, and cross-border Dubrovnik trips.
aboutPage.p1: Car Rental Herceg Novi is a booking platform partnered with small and mid-sized rental operators on Montenegro's western coast, the stretch from the Debeli Brijeg border through Igalo, Herceg Novi, and onto the Kamenari ferry. We're a small local team, not a global franchise.
aboutPage.p2: Herceg Novi sits at the western gateway to the Bay of Kotor, between the Croatian border and the Verige Strait. It's the natural base for visitors who want two-country access without moving hotels: Dubrovnik is 40 minutes west, Kotor 30 minutes east.
aboutPage.p3Mid2: is a short ferry away, and the
aboutPage.p3After: delivers a mountain drive forty minutes from the beach.
aboutPage.p4: CDW and third-party liability insurance, VAT, unlimited mileage on most categories, winter tyres November through March on mountain-class vehicles, and 24/7 roadside support. Zero-excess upgrade available at booking or pickup. Cross-border cover for Croatia, Bosnia, Serbia, Albania and Kosovo is included when requested.
aboutPage.p5Before: We meet every flight at Tivat Airport (TIV), 40 minutes east via the
aboutPage.p5After: or around the bay. We can also arrange Dubrovnik Airport pickup for travellers flying into Croatia, with cross-border delivery included. In-town pickups are available at your Herceg Novi or Igalo hotel.
aboutPage.p6: No summer premium on the confirmation page. The rate you book is the rate you pay.
aboutPage.p7After: for form, email, and WhatsApp. We reply within two hours during business hours.

montenegroPage.title: Driving Montenegro from the Western Bay
montenegroPage.subtitle: The country from Herceg Novi as a base, two-country access and the quickest route to every major destination
montenegroPage.description: Overview of Montenegro for Herceg Novi-based drivers: typical drive times to Kotor, Budva, Podgorica, and across the Croatian border.
montenegroPage.h2Base: Herceg Novi as a base, the two-country advantage
montenegroPage.p1: Herceg Novi is the westernmost town on the Montenegrin coast. It's the only base from which you can do a morning in Croatia and an afternoon back in Montenegro without packing a bag. That geographical position defines what Montenegro looks like from here.
montenegroPage.li2: Kotor Old Town, 45 min around the bay
montenegroPage.p2: In a week from Herceg Novi you can reasonably tick off the Croatian walled city, a Montenegrin old town, a mountain hike, a beach day, a monastery morning, and a border-crossing into Bosnia, all without moving accommodation. Few Mediterranean bases offer that kind of geographic density.
montenegroPage.borderCroatiaBefore: 15 min to the border. Queue variable, see our
montenegroPage.borderBosniaBefore: 20 min to the border, then straight into Trebinje country, see the
montenegroPage.borderAlbaniaText: requires an hour through coastal Montenegro first, then the Božaj crossing near Podgorica.
montenegroPage.p3: May-June and September are ideal, warm sea, mild interior, shorter border queues. July-August is hot, busy, and the border waits peak. October-April is quieter but most tourist infrastructure scales back.

aboutBody.h1: Rental Cars Matched to Outer-Bay Roads
aboutBody.p1: Car Rental Herceg Novi partners with trusted local operators to offer a fleet suited to outer-bay conditions: compact hatchbacks for the stepped lanes of the Old Town and central Šetalište Pet Danica, SUVs for the Orjen road to Ledenice, and premium saloons for cross-border runs to Dubrovnik and Trebinje. Every vehicle is supplied by established operators around the bay, serviced regularly, and delivered with full insurance.
aboutBody.h2: Why a Car Changes Everything Here
aboutBody.p2: Scheduled buses follow the magistrala and stop. That is the extent of public transport. They will not detour to Rose on the Luštica peninsula, nor climb to Ledenice on Orjen at 700 m, nor reach the back road down to Žanjice. They cannot time a crossing on the Kamenari–Lepetane ferry or pause at the Savina monastery above Igalo. Only your own wheels and a willingness to explore unlock the outer bay properly.
aboutBody.p3: We offer collection across the outer bay: central Herceg Novi, Igalo, Bijela, Đenovići, Kumbor (Portonovi), Tivat Airport (40 minutes east) and Dubrovnik Airport (40 minutes west across the Croatian border). Economy hatchbacks thread through Old Town approach roads effortlessly. SUVs handle the Orjen switchbacks without breaking stride. Every vehicle carries full insurance, and cross-border documentation for Croatia, Bosnia, Albania, Serbia, and Kosovo is arranged at the time of booking.
aboutBody.promise1: Full insurance with a zero-excess upgrade on every reservation
aboutBody.promise2: A named representative at Tivat or Dubrovnik arrivals, sign in hand, vehicle parked outside
aboutBody.promise3: Transparent pricing, no airport levies, no fuel-policy surprises
aboutBody.promise4: Green Card paperwork arranged for five neighbouring countries
aboutBody.promise5: Free cancellation up to seven days before your collection date
aboutBody.promise6: Round-the-clock roadside support, from Herceg Novi to Žabljak and everywhere in between
aboutBody.p4: Ask us which mornings the Kamenari ferry queue builds, we can advise you to cross before 09:00 or loop inland via Trebinje instead. Ask about the Orjen trails and we can point you toward the Ledenice viewpoint and the Subra ridge. From the Debeli Brijeg checkpoint timings to the quietest hour for Žanjice beach, we draw on local knowledge from partners and drivers working across the outer bay.
aboutBody.p5: WhatsApp, Viber, a traditional phone call, whichever suits you. We respond quickly, even during the hectic July cruise-ship season. Browse the fleet online, lock in a rate, and we take care of the rest. Or message us a question about parking near the Cathedral, the best route to Cetinje, or whether that mountain pass is open in December.
aboutBody.h6: Our Vehicles and What They Handle
aboutBody.p6: Each car in the fleet is chosen for the realities of Montenegrin roads. The compact hatchbacks, Fiat Panda, VW Polo, Renault Clio, slot into the narrow lane along Marshal Tito in Herceg Novi where larger vehicles simply cannot fit. For families or longer trips, the mid-size SUVs (Dacia Duster, Nissan Qashqai) carry luggage comfortably and handle the loose gravel on the Orjen road without hesitation. Premium saloons suit the motorway run to Podgorica via Nikšić or the cross-border highway to Dubrovnik. Vehicles are serviced by our partner operators in Herceg Novi and Bijela, checked between rentals, and delivered with a full tank of fuel and verified tyre pressures for mountain driving.
aboutBody.h7: A Typical Week Behind the Wheel
aboutBody.p7: Monday morning: collect in central Herceg Novi, walk the 100,000 Steps, afternoon coffee on the waterfront. Tuesday: Kamenari ferry across the Verige strait, day in Perast with the Our Lady of the Rocks boat, loop back through Risan for the Roman mosaics. Wednesday: the Orjen road up to Ledenice for the outer-bay panorama, then down to Igalo for the mud-bath spa. Thursday: cross at Debeli Brijeg in the morning, spend the afternoon in Dubrovnik, back for dinner in the Old Town. Friday: Luštica loop to Rose and Žanjice, short ferry across to Mamula Island. Saturday: inland via the Sutorina valley to Trebinje for Bosnian lunch. Sunday: return the car. That entire circuit covers roughly 450 km. Fuel cost: approximately 45 to 55 euros at current diesel prices. No bus, tour group, or taxi could replicate it at any price.
aboutBody.p8: Summer brings the cruise ships calling at Kotor, up to four vessels per day between June and September, and with them passengers spilling out across the bay. Herceg Novi stays quieter, its harbour is too shallow for the big ships, but the Kamenari ferry queue and the Debeli Brijeg border both build from mid-morning. We advise an early start or a late-evening cross. Winter transforms the outer bay into a quieter place: fewer visitors, lower rates, and Orjen roads that occasionally close above 900 metres due to snow. Spring and autumn are the sweet spot. The Mimosa Festival in February paints Igalo yellow, September still offers warm seas without the crowds, and the Orjen road is open and uncrowded.

montenegroBody.h1: Driving Montenegro from a Herceg Novi Base
montenegroBody.p1: Montenegro is roughly the size of Northern Ireland yet contains a coast, a deep fjord-like bay, walled towns from the Venetian period, a 1,300-metre-deep canyon, and alpine peaks above 2,500 metres. Driving distances look short on a map. In practice, the roads twist, climb, tunnel, and ferry-cross their way between these extremes, and journey times rarely match what GPS predicts. Accept that, and the driving itself becomes part of the holiday.
montenegroBody.p2: Herceg Novi sits at the western mouth of the Bay of Kotor, 25 km from the Croatian border at Debeli Brijeg and a short Kamenari ferry hop from the inner bay. Dubrovnik is 40 minutes west. Kotor is 45 minutes east via the ferry. Ledenice on Orjen is 45 minutes inland. Lake Skadar is two hours south. Only Durmitor, in the far north, requires a genuine road trip. Everything else radiates outward from the Old Town like spokes from a hub.
montenegroBody.startText: Tivat Airport (TIV) is 40 minutes east via the Kamenari–Lepetane ferry. Dubrovnik Airport (DBV) is 40 minutes west across the Debeli Brijeg crossing in Croatia. Podgorica Airport offers wider winter schedules but adds two hours of inland driving. Herceg Novi is unusual in Montenegro for having two international airports within equal reach, pick whichever has the better fare. All three points support meet-and-greet handovers.
montenegroBody.timesTitle: Realistic Drive Times from Herceg Novi
montenegroBody.timesText1: Igalo: 10 min. Kamenari ferry: 20 min. Dubrovnik border (Debeli Brijeg): 35 min. Tivat Airport: 40 min. Dubrovnik: 40 min. Kotor: 45 min. Ledenice (Orjen): 45 min. Trebinje (Bosnia): 1 h 15. Podgorica: 2 h. Žabljak (Durmitor): 3.5–4 h.
montenegroBody.timesText2: The Orjen switchbacks up to Ledenice contain a dozen tight hairpins across 22 km of climbing. Navigation apps routinely underestimate mountain stretches by 20–30%. Plan accordingly and carry water.
montenegroBody.rulesIntro: Random roadside checks are common, especially on the magistrala and near border crossings. Officers will ask for:
montenegroBody.rule1: A valid driving licence (international driving permit accepted alongside it)
montenegroBody.rule2: The original rental contract (photocopies are not sufficient)
montenegroBody.rule3: Proof of insurance (the rental company provides this in the vehicle)
montenegroBody.rule4: A Green Card if crossing any border (approximately 15 euros for 15 days)
montenegroBody.keyRule1: Seat belts: mandatory for every occupant, front and rear
montenegroBody.keyRule2: Mobile phones: hands-free only, and even then police may stop you
montenegroBody.keyRule3: Alcohol: zero-tolerance policy, any detectable blood alcohol means an on-the-spot fine or worse
montenegroBody.keyRule4: Speed cameras: fixed units on the magistrala and mobile traps on the highway. Fines are issued to the rental company and charged back to your card
montenegroBody.conditionsTitle: What the Roads Are Actually Like
montenegroBody.conditionsText: The magistrala between Herceg Novi and Kotor is paved and well maintained but barely two cars wide in places, with stone walls on one side and the Bay of Kotor on the other. Mountain roads above 800 metres, the Orjen climb in particular, may lack guard rails entirely. The Sozina tunnel toward Bar is modern and well-lit. The Ledenice road is narrow, steep, and has limited passing places, so honk before blind bends. In winter, chains or winter tyres are legally required above the snow line.
montenegroBody.e65Title: E65 / E80: The Adriatic Coast Road
montenegroBody.e65Text: This route traces the Adriatic coast from the Debeli Brijeg crossing west of Herceg Novi, around the mouth of the Bay of Kotor, over the Kamenari–Lepetane car ferry, and onward past Tivat toward the southern coast. The ferry shortcut across the Verige strait saves 45 minutes versus the loop via Risan. The outer-bay magistrala is where most Herceg Novi visitors spend their driving time, and for good reason, the views change with every headland.
montenegroBody.e762Text: Cuts inland from the Adriatic through the Montenegrin heartland toward the Serbian and eastern Bosnian borders. This is the route to Niksic, the Piva canyon, and eventually Durmitor. Less scenic near the coast but increasingly dramatic as it climbs.
montenegroBody.borderText: Cross-border driving is permitted with a Green Card. The Debeli Brijeg crossing into Croatia sits 25 km west of Herceg Novi and is by far the most convenient for an HN base, and the busiest. In July and August, waits of one to two hours are normal. Weekday mornings before 08:00 or evenings after 20:00 cut the queue significantly. The inland Ivanica crossing into Bosnia, reached via the Sutorina valley, is almost always quieter. Albania, Serbia, and Kosovo crossings are further afield but generally fast.
montenegroBody.nationText: Independent only since 2006, Montenegro remains lightly visited compared to Croatia or Greece. The roads are quieter, the parking easier, and the landscapes more concentrated. A single tank of fuel can take you from Herceg Novi's stepped lanes to a glacial mountain lake and back. Few countries this small pack this much into their borders.
montenegroBody.fuelP1: Montenegro has three main fuel brands: Jugopetrol (part of Hellenic Petroleum), Eko, and several independent stations. Prices are regulated and relatively uniform across the country at approximately 1.45 to 1.55 euros per litre for diesel and slightly more for petrol. All major stations accept credit cards, though smaller independent stations in the north occasionally require cash.
montenegroBody.fuelP2: On the coast, fuel stations are frequent, you will pass one every 15 to 20 km along the magistrala. In the mountains, they thin out considerably. Fill your tank in Herceg Novi or Igalo before heading up Orjen to Ledenice, inland to Trebinje, or anywhere in the northern highlands. The Grahovo highland stretch and the old mountain route between Cetinje and Podgorica have no fuel stops at all.
montenegroBody.speedP1: Built-up areas: 50 km/h. Open roads: 80 km/h. Dual carriageways and motorway-standard roads: 100 km/h (or 130 km/h where posted). The magistrala fluctuates between 40 and 60 km/h depending on the settlement. Fixed speed cameras sit at several points along the outer-bay magistrala, near Igalo, near Risan, and at the entrance to the Sozina tunnel.
montenegroBody.speedP2: Mobile police speed traps appear randomly, particularly on the highway between the coast and Podgorica and on the approach to border crossings. Fines are issued on the spot and range from 30 to 200 euros depending on how far over the limit you were travelling. The rental company is notified electronically if a camera catches you, and the fine is charged to your credit card.
montenegroBody.mountainP1: Montenegrin mountain roads are beautiful and demanding in equal measure. The Orjen road from the coast up to Ledenice climbs 700 metres in 22 km with tight hairpins, rough-patched asphalt, and no guard rails on the upper sections. The road to Durmitor via the Piva canyon is 170 km of continuous mountain driving with narrow bridges, unlit tunnels, and occasional rockfall debris.
montenegroBody.mountainP2: Practical advice: use low gears on descents to save your brakes. Honk before blind bends, it is standard practice and potentially lifesaving. Carry at least two litres of water per person. Keep headlights on even in daytime. Pull into passing places to let faster or larger vehicles through. And budget 30 per cent more time than your GPS suggests.
montenegroBody.startLink: Start at {{link}}, the closest airport to Herceg Novi via the Kamenari ferry.

borderCrossingBody.h1: Taking a Rental Car Across Balkan Borders
borderCrossingBody.p1: Montenegro shares land borders with five countries: Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Albania, Kosovo, and Serbia. From a Herceg Novi base, Croatia is closer than most Montenegrin towns, the Debeli Brijeg crossing sits 25 km west, a 35-minute drive. Driving a rental car into any neighbouring country is permitted, provided you inform the rental company at booking so the paperwork is ready when you collect the keys.
borderCrossingBody.greenCardText: A Green Card (Carte Internationale d'Assurance Automobile) is a standardised document proving your vehicle carries valid third-party insurance in the country you are entering. Border guards request it at every Balkan checkpoint. Without one, you will be turned around.
borderCrossingBody.greenCard1: Typical cost: around 15 euros for a 15-day period
borderCrossingBody.greenCard2: Issued by: the rental company, at no hassle to you, just confirm the countries you plan to visit when booking
borderCrossingBody.greenCard3: Standard coverage: Croatia, Bosnia, Albania, Serbia. Kosovo may need to be added separately
borderCrossingBody.greenCardWarn: Before setting off, open the glovebox and verify the Green Card lists every country on your route. A card valid for Croatia alone will not get you into Bosnia. Five minutes of checking saves hours of frustration at the checkpoint.
borderCrossingBody.doc1: Passport or EU national ID card (must be original, not a photocopy)
borderCrossingBody.doc2: Driving licence, an international driving permit is recommended alongside non-Latin-script licences
borderCrossingBody.doc3: Original rental contract with company stamp (printed, not a screenshot on your phone)
borderCrossingBody.doc4: Green Card insurance certificate listing all destination countries
borderCrossingBody.doc5: Vehicle registration certificate (kept in the car by the rental company)
borderCrossingBody.croatiaText: The E65 coastal crossing between Herceg Novi and Dubrovnik is by far the most popular from this side of the bay, and therefore the most congested. It sits just 25 km, about 35 minutes, from the Herceg Novi waterfront. In July and August, two passport checks (Montenegrin exit, Croatian entry) combine with heavy tourist traffic to produce queues of one to two hours. The crossing itself is a simple road checkpoint with booths on both sides.
borderCrossingBody.croatiaTip: Timing matters enormously. Cross before 08:00 or after 20:00 and the wait drops to minutes. Weekday mornings are dramatically quieter than Saturday or Sunday. If you must cross mid-afternoon in August, bring water and patience, or loop inland via Ivanica and the Sutorina valley into Bosnia, then drop down to Dubrovnik from the north, which often ends up faster.
borderCrossingBody.croatiaFerry: Heading east from Herceg Novi toward Kotor, the Kamenari–Lepetane car ferry crosses the narrowest point of the bay in about five minutes, saving a 45-minute drive around the inner shore via Risan. Ferries run continuously through the day; the fare is a few euros per vehicle.
borderCrossingBody.albaniaTitle: Into Albania: Sukobin or Hani i Hotit
borderCrossingBody.albaniaText: The coastal crossing at Sukobin/Muriqan, south of Ulcinj, is the more straightforward route, well-signed, with generally short queues. The inland alternative at Hani i Hotit, near the southern shore of Lake Skadar, is scenic but quieter and less familiar to foreign tourists.
borderCrossingBody.albaniaNote: Albanian road infrastructure is improving rapidly but remains unpredictable outside main highways. Speed limits drop on rural roads and surface quality can change without warning. Drive defensively, particularly at night.
borderCrossingBody.bosniaTitle: Into Bosnia: Ivanica (Sutorina) or Šćepan Polje
borderCrossingBody.bosniaText: The Ivanica crossing, 20 minutes inland from Herceg Novi up the Sutorina valley, is the easiest Bosnian route on this side of Montenegro, Trebinje is only 45 minutes from the HN waterfront, making it a viable half-day lunch trip. Further north, the Šćepan Polje crossing near Foča accesses the Tara River gorge and Sutjeska National Park. Both are lightly used and rarely involve waiting.
borderCrossingBody.serbiaText: The Dobrakovo checkpoint on the E65, north of Bijelo Polje, connects to the Serbian motorway network. From there, Belgrade is roughly four hours of straightforward motorway driving. Tolls are charged at motorway entry and exit booths, take a ticket when you join and pay based on distance when you leave.
borderCrossingBody.kosovoText: The Kula crossing near Rozaje leads to Peja/Pec in western Kosovo. Confirm with your rental company that Kosovo is explicitly listed on your Green Card, several insurers exclude it by default, and border guards will check.
borderCrossingBody.tip1: Arrange documents in a single envelope on the dashboard before joining the queue. Fumbling at the window wastes time
borderCrossingBody.tip2: Switch off the engine while stationary, guards notice and it puts them at ease
borderCrossingBody.tip3: Avoid the Debeli Brijeg crossing on Saturday and Sunday afternoons between June and September
borderCrossingBody.tip4: Inland crossings (Bosnia, Serbia, Kosovo) are almost always faster than the coastal Croatian one
borderCrossingBody.tip5: Carry euros in cash. Croatian motorway tolls accept cards, but Bosnian and Albanian fuel stations sometimes do not
borderCrossingBody.tip6: Check your mobile roaming plan before crossing. Data charges spike the moment your phone connects to a foreign network
borderCrossingBody.lawsTitle: Traffic Laws That Change at the Border
borderCrossingBody.lawsIntro: Regulations differ from one country to the next. The main surprises:
borderCrossingBody.law1: Croatia: daytime running lights mandatory at all times. Motorway tolls are collected by cash or card at barrier stations
borderCrossingBody.law2: Albania: lower speed limits on secondary roads than in Montenegro. Livestock on the carriageway is not uncommon outside towns
borderCrossingBody.law3: Bosnia: winter tyres compulsory from November through April. A first-aid kit and warning triangle must be in the vehicle
borderCrossingBody.law4: Serbia: absolute zero-tolerance alcohol limit. Motorways use toll booths, take a ticket on entry, pay on exit based on distance
borderCrossingBody.law5: Kosovo: right-hand traffic, same as Montenegro. An international driving permit is strongly recommended
borderCrossingBody.popularTitle: Common Multi-Country Itineraries from Herceg Novi
borderCrossingBody.route1: Herceg Novi to Dubrovnik: 40 minutes on the coastal E65, one border at Debeli Brijeg, and arguably the finest short coastal drive in the Mediterranean
borderCrossingBody.route2: Herceg Novi to Trebinje: 1 h 15 via the Sutorina valley and the Ivanica crossing. Ottoman quarter, Hercegovačka Gračanica monastery, and Bosnian lunch prices
borderCrossingBody.route3: Herceg Novi to Mostar: 3 hours via Trebinje. The Stari Most bridge and Ottoman quarter are the draw
borderCrossingBody.route4: Herceg Novi to Shkodër: roughly 4 hours via Podgorica and along Lake Skadar. A different country, a different pace entirely
borderCrossingBody.returnP: The return crossing is usually faster than the outbound one, especially at Debeli Brijeg. Montenegrin border officers are generally efficient and rarely hold up traffic. Make sure you still have all documents easily accessible, the Green Card, rental contract, and passport will be checked again. Refuel before crossing back into Montenegro if you have been driving in Croatia, as Croatian fuel prices tend to be slightly higher.
borderCrossingBody.ctaText: Looking for a car that comes with cross-border paperwork already sorted? Search our fleet, tick the countries you plan to visit at booking, and the Green Card will be in the glovebox when you collect the keys.
borderCrossingBody.croatiaFerryLink: Hiring from {{link}} is the most popular cross-border option.

blogIndex.heroSubtitle: Road routes, local secrets, and quiet corners of the outer Bay of Kotor, from people who drive these roads daily.
blogIndex.pageTitle: Driving the Western Bay of Kotor
blogIndex.pageSubtitle: Researched guides to the quieter corners of the Herceg Novi region, ferries, villages, viewpoints and the borders behind the old town.
blogIndex.card_churches_excerpt: The car ferry across the Verige strait saves a 45-minute drive around the inner bay. How often it runs, what it costs, and the morning queue patterns worth planning around.
blogIndex.card_vrmac_title: Rose Village: The Far Tip of Luštica
blogIndex.card_vrmac_excerpt: A 45-minute drive from Herceg Novi via the Kamenari ferry drops you in a stone fishing village with no through road. Konoba lunches, Mamula views, and the quietest swimming on the outer bay.
blogIndex.card_markets_excerpt: Switchbacks from the magistrala climb to 700 m on Orjen mountain. Where to park, what the road surface is actually like, and why you want to be there before 10:00.
blogIndex.card_swimming_title: Risan Roman Mosaics: A Short Drive Worth Making
blogIndex.card_swimming_excerpt: Twenty minutes past Kamenari, the Hellenistic-Roman mosaics at Risan sit in an unmarked shed that most cruise passengers never reach. Combine with a Perast lunch for a perfect half-day.
blogIndex.card_cetinje_title: Portonovi Marina and the Podi Hill Villages
blogIndex.card_cetinje_excerpt: Kumbor's superyacht marina is the glamour stop, but the medieval hamlets on the Podi hill behind it are the real reason to drive this stretch. A loop guide from Herceg Novi.
blogIndex.card_dobrota_title: Bijela Shipyards: Herceg Novi's Working Coast
blogIndex.card_dobrota_excerpt: Bijela's dry dock has been patching Adriatic freighters since 1927. What the yard looks like now, why the shoreline views are better than Instagram suggests, and where to eat locally.
blogIndex.card_kayak_title: Đenovići: The Promenade Locals Walk at Sunset
blogIndex.card_kayak_excerpt: Herceg Novi's western flank runs through Đenovići with a two-kilometre seaside promenade, stepped swimming platforms, and konobas that don't appear in any guidebook.
blogIndex.card_stoliv_title: Into Bosnia via the Sutorina Valley
blogIndex.card_stoliv_excerpt: The quiet inland crossing at Ivanica leads up the Sutorina valley to Trebinje in 45 minutes. Green Card essentials, fuel stops, and why it's the Bosnian lunch most Boka visitors miss.
blogIndex.card_wildflowers_title: Tivat or Dubrovnik? Choosing Your HN Airport
blogIndex.card_wildflowers_excerpt: Herceg Novi is the only Montenegrin base within 40 minutes of two international airports. Route comparisons, border timing, and which you actually want for a morning arrival.

blogHome.sectionSubtitle: Road routes, quiet corners, and local tips for exploring the outer Bay of Kotor by car.

cookieBody.whatText: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the site remember your preferences and understand how you use it.
cookieBody.essentialText: These are necessary for the website to function. They cannot be disabled.
cookieBody.sessionDesc: maintain your browsing session while you navigate the site
cookieBody.preferenceDesc: remember your language and location selections
cookieBody.thirdPartyText: Our car rental booking widget (provided by LocalRent) may set cookies to:
cookieBody.thirdParty2: Maintain your session during the booking process
cookieBody.thirdPartyNote: These cookies are set by LocalRent and are governed by their privacy policy.
cookieBody.managingText: You can control cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to:
cookieBody.managingNote: Note that blocking essential cookies may affect the functionality of the booking widget.
cookieBody.contactText: Questions about our use of cookies? Contact us at info@carrentalhercegnovi.com.

privacyBody.whoText: Car Rental Herceg Novi operates as an affiliate partner connecting travellers with car rental providers in and around Herceg Novi, Montenegro. Our website is located at carrentalhercegnovi.com. When you use our site, we may collect certain information as described below.
privacyBody.provideText: When you use our booking search form, we collect the pickup location, dates, and times you enter in order to find available vehicles. This information is passed to our partner (LocalRent) to generate search results. We do not collect or store payment details, all payments are processed directly by the rental provider.
privacyBody.auto1: pages visited, time spent on site, referring website
privacyBody.auto2: browser type, operating system, screen resolution
privacyBody.auto3: used for approximate location and security purposes
privacyBody.how1: To connect you with car rental availability and pricing
privacyBody.how2: To improve our website and user experience
privacyBody.how3: To understand how visitors use our site (analytics)
privacyBody.how4: To respond to enquiries via WhatsApp, Viber, or email
privacyBody.thirdText: Our website uses the following third-party services:
privacyBody.third1: car rental booking widget, processes your search queries and handles all booking and payment
privacyBody.thirdNote: Each of these services has their own privacy policy governing how they handle data.
privacyBody.cookiesText: We use minimal cookies necessary for the website to function. The LocalRent booking widget may set its own cookies for session management and preferences. See our {{link}} for details.
privacyBody.right1: Request access to any personal data we hold about you
privacyBody.right2: Request correction or deletion of your data
privacyBody.right4: Lodge a complaint with a data protection authority
privacyBody.contactText: For any privacy-related questions, contact us via email at info@carrentalhercegnovi.com.

termsBody.aboutText: Car Rental Herceg Novi (carrentalhercegnovi.com) is an affiliate website that connects travellers with car rental services in and around Herceg Novi, Montenegro. We are not a car rental company, we partner with LocalRent to provide real-time availability and booking services.
termsBody.bookingP1: All car rental bookings made through our website are processed and fulfilled by our partner, LocalRent. When you complete a booking, you enter into a rental agreement directly with the vehicle supplier, not with Car Rental Herceg Novi.
termsBody.bookingP2: The rental terms, including cancellation policies, insurance coverage, age requirements, and payment conditions, are set by the vehicle supplier and displayed during the booking process. We recommend reading these terms carefully before confirming any booking.
termsBody.provide1: A search interface to browse available rental vehicles
termsBody.provide2: Destination and travel information about Herceg Novi and the outer Bay of Kotor region
termsBody.provide3: Customer support to help with enquiries before booking
termsBody.doNot1: Own, operate, or maintain any rental vehicles
termsBody.doNot3: Set rental prices, insurance terms, or cancellation policies
termsBody.pricingText: Prices displayed on our website are provided in real-time by our booking partner and are subject to change. The final price is confirmed at the time of booking. All prices include VAT and mandatory insurance unless otherwise stated.
termsBody.contentText: We make every effort to ensure that information on this website is accurate and up to date. However, travel information, driving regulations, and border crossing requirements can change. We recommend verifying critical details (especially border crossing documents and driving rules) with official sources before your trip.
termsBody.liabilityText: Car Rental Herceg Novi acts as an intermediary and is not liable for:
termsBody.liability1: The condition or availability of rental vehicles
termsBody.liability2: Actions or omissions by rental suppliers
termsBody.liability3: Losses arising from border crossing issues or documentation problems
termsBody.liability4: Changes to prices after booking confirmation
termsBody.ipText: All content on this website, including text, design, and graphics, is owned by Car Rental Herceg Novi unless otherwise credited. Stock photography is used under licence
termsBody.contactText: Questions about these terms? Contact us at info@carrentalhercegnovi.com.

affiliate.heroSubtitle: Join the Car Rental Herceg Novi affiliate programme. Promote car rental in Montenegro and earn an average of €21 per referral, with no cap on earnings.
affiliate.step1Text: Register through our partner LocalRent. Approval is instant, no minimum traffic requirements.
affiliate.step2Text: Promote using your unique referral link, an embeddable booking widget, white-label solution, or API integration.
affiliate.step3Text: Every confirmed car rental earns you 50% of the platform commission. Payouts from €10 minimum.
affiliate.revenueShareText: You earn half of every booking commission, including insurance add-ons, which can reach 50% of the rental price.
affiliate.avgBookingText: Montenegro rentals average €21 commission per confirmed reservation. High-season bookings earn more.
affiliate.noCapText: There is no upper limit. The more bookings you refer, the more you earn. Top affiliates earn €1,000+/month.
affiliate.minPayoutText: Request your earnings from just €10. Payments processed via your affiliate dashboard.
affiliate.refLinksText: Simple tracking links for blog posts, social media, newsletters, and any web property you operate.
affiliate.widgetText: Embed a fully functional car search and booking widget directly on your website. Customisable colours and layout.
affiliate.whiteLabelText: Run the entire booking experience on your own domain. White-label increases conversion by up to 2.7× compared to standard links.
affiliate.apiText: Full API access for developers who want to build custom booking flows, price comparison tools, or mobile apps.
affiliate.whoBloggersText: Write about Montenegro, the Balkans, or European road trips? Your readers are already looking for rental cars.
affiliate.whoLocalText: Hotels, apartments, tour operators, and transfer companies in Montenegro, recommend cars to your guests.
affiliate.whoCreatorsText: YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, share your outer-bay or Dubrovnik-border driving content and monetise with rental links.
affiliate.whoDevsText: Build travel tools, comparison sites, or booking platforms using the API and white-label solution.
affiliate.ctaSubtitle: Free to join. No minimum traffic. 50% commission from day one.
affiliate.title: Affiliate Programme
affiliate.seoDesc: Earn 50% commission on every car rental booking in Montenegro. Join the Car Rental Herceg Novi affiliate programme.

popularRoutes.label: Popular Routes
popularRoutes.title: Scenic Drives from Herceg Novi
popularRoutes.subtitle: Four roads worth renting a car for.

0.title: Herceg Novi to Perast via Kamenari Ferry
0.desc: Magistrala to Kamenari, five-minute roll-on ferry, then the inner bay road to Perast and Our Lady of the Rocks.

1.title: Herceg Novi to Kotor
1.desc: Via the Kamenari–Lepetane ferry to save 40 minutes around the bay. walls and the climb to San Giovanni await.

2.title: Herceg Novi to Ledenice on Orjen
2.desc: Switchback climb from the coast to 700 m on Orjen mountain. Windows open, second gear, and the outer bay spreading out behind you.

3.title: Herceg Novi to Dubrovnik
3.desc: Coastal E65 through Igalo, Debeli Brijeg border, Konavle valley. Green Card in the glovebox, passports on the dashboard.

insiderTips.label: Outer Bay Wisdom
insiderTips.title: Three Rules for the Outer Bay

0.title: Mimosa Festival Weekend Books Out
0.text: The February Mimosa Festival fills every hotel and rental in Herceg Novi for a single weekend. If you’re arriving in late Feb, book the car the same week you book the hotel, supply is thin.

1.title: The Žanjice Descent Is Brake-Light Country
1.text: The back road down to Žanjice and Mirišta on Luštica is steep, tight and full of blind corners. Second gear, windows open for echo, and take every pull-off offered by oncoming traffic.

2.title: Croatia in 40 Minutes
2.text: Debeli Brijeg is the closest Montenegro–Croatia crossing in the country, 40 minutes from the Herceg Novi waterfront. Green Card in the glovebox, passports on the dashboard, and you’re on the road to Dubrovnik inside the hour.

experiences.title: Things to Do with a Rental Car
experiences.subtitle: Every destination is a drive away, no tour bus, no schedule, just you and the road.

Bus from Dubrovnik: two changes and a border, half a day gone
Stuck to Kamenari ferry foot-passenger timings
Can't reach Žanjice, Rose, Ledenice or Trebinje

Cross-border to Croatia in 35 min: permitted

contentPage.readyCta: Ready to drive the outer Bay of Kotor?

cookieBanner.title: Cookie Policy
cookieBanner.text: We use cookies to improve your browsing experience.

renault-clio.name: Renault Clio
renault-clio.tagline: The low-maintenance coast-runner for a fortnight-long Herceg Novi base
renault-clio.lede: Light on fuel, cheap to park at the pickup location, and comfortable on the motorway push to Podgorica or Žabljak.
renault-clio.suitability: A pair settling in Herceg Novi for ten to fourteen days, with regular day-trips out to Cetinje, Risan and the Lake Skadar shore, simple to park, forgiving on petrol.
renault-clio.regional: Handles the Herceg Novi–Cetinje climb in third gear without drama, threads the narrow waterfront through outlying villages, and the 5.3 L/100 km petrol keeps the fuel bill predictable over a multi-week hire.
renault-clio.inlineAlt: Bay of Kotor coastal road near Dobrota
renault-clio.inlineCaption: The Dobrota–Prčanj stretch at a steady 50 km/h, the Clio's natural cruising pace on a multi-day bay stay.

peugeot-308.name: Peugeot 308
peugeot-308.tagline: Diesel mid-size for the Herceg Novi → Dubrovnik and Herceg Novi → Durmitor runs
peugeot-308.lede: Long-legged BlueHDi + auto box built for the 3-hour push up to Žabljak or the cross-border drive to Trebinje and Mostar.
peugeot-308.suitability: The mid-size diesel picks for anyone pairing a Herceg Novi base with multi-day drives inland, auto gearbox and cruise control earn their keep on the Podgorica motorway.
peugeot-308.regional: Cruises the Smokovac–Mateševo motorway section effortlessly, swallows a week of hiking kit bound for Žabljak, and covers the Herceg Novi → Dubrovnik via Debeli Brijeg run on half a tank.
peugeot-308.inlineCaption: The Smokovac motorway toward Podgorica, the 308's BlueHDi settles into a steady 1,800 rpm cruise here.

renault-megane.name: Renault Megane
renault-megane.tagline: Biggest-boot French mid-size, built for long-stay loads and lake trips
renault-megane.lede: 434-litre boot, quiet diesel cruising, sofa-soft damping, the mid-size wagon pick for a month in the bay with regular excursions out.
renault-megane.suitability: Renters who want more than a hatchback's packing space without the length of a full estate, ideal for Skadar Lake fishing kit, Cetinje monastery day-bags and a cool-box for the drive home.
renault-megane.regional: The long-travel suspension shrugs off the patchy bitumen on the old Herceg Novi–Budva road, the dCi diesel sips on the Sozina tunnel run, and the generous boot takes two weeks of family kit plus the laundry bag you pick up in Prčanj.
renault-megane.inlineAlt: Old bay road from Kotor toward Risan
renault-megane.inlineCaption: The patched tarmac out to Risan's Roman mosaics, where the Megane's soft damping actually earns its rental premium.

peugeot-208.name: Peugeot 208
peugeot-208.tagline: Small-footprint French hatch, the cheapest way onto the Herceg Novi waterfront
peugeot-208.lede: Lightest, cheapest runaround in the fleet. i-Cockpit gauges, PureTech 1.2 petrol, and a small enough footprint to slip into the bastion car-parks at the Sea Gate.
peugeot-208.suitability: The cheapest economy slot in the fleet, for a short 3- to 5-night stay with daily coffee runs to the Old Town and afternoon drives out to Žanjic and Mirište.
peugeot-208.regional: The 4.05 m length parks in the same bays a Clio takes, the 311-litre boot swallows two carry-ons plus a cool-box, and the 4.7 L/100 km petrol keeps a week of Kotor-Perast pottering under €25 of fuel.

hyundai-i20.name: Hyundai i20
hyundai-i20.tagline: Quiet Korean hatch, the refined choice in the small-car class
hyundai-i20.lede: Plusher cabin than the French rivals, refined 1.2 MPi petrol, lane-keep and AEB as standard. The small-car pick when you want a quieter drive than a Clio or Fiesta.
hyundai-i20.suitability: A soft, unflustered small hatch for renters who want modern safety kit without paying mid-size prices. Ideal week-long Herceg Novi base with trips to Perast, Tivat, Risan.
hyundai-i20.regional: The 4-cylinder engine is noticeably smoother than the 3-pot alternatives on the long motorway push to Podgorica, and lane-keep assist takes the edge off the tight cambered sections through Morinj and Kostanjica.

toyota-yaris.name: Toyota Yaris
toyota-yaris.tagline: Reliable Toyota hatch, the 'never breaks down' option for longer road trips
toyota-yaris.lede: The small-car pick if reliability matters more than badge cachet. 1.5 four-cylinder with Toyota's legendary service intervals, zero worry even on a three-week rental.
toyota-yaris.suitability: For renters who pick the car on past reliability rather than styling, the Yaris is the most forgiving small hatch on the Herceg Novi → Durmitor → Podgorica → back loop.
toyota-yaris.regional: 125 hp has genuine pace for the Lovćen climb, the 3.94 m length is the shortest in the economy lineup aside from the Ignis, and Toyota Safety Sense manages the sudden lane-merges on the Sozina tunnel approach.

ford-fiesta.name: Ford Fiesta
ford-fiesta.tagline: Ford's best-handling small car, the pick for the Herceg Novi → Lovćen hairpins
ford-fiesta.lede: The enthusiast's small hatch. 1.0 EcoBoost 3-cyl makes real pace, and Ford's chassis tuning turns the Lovćen switchbacks into a highlight rather than an endurance test.
ford-fiesta.suitability: A small car that actually rewards driving. For renters whose itinerary includes the Lovćen 25-bends, the cross-bay coast road through Kostanjica, or the winding back route via Grahovo.
ford-fiesta.regional: The 100 hp turbo hauls cleanly out of the Herceg Novi bastion tunnels, six-speed gearing keeps the engine in its sweet spot on the E65 grade, and the precise steering is the real reason to pick a Fiesta over a Clio for the mountain drives.

suzuki-ignis.name: Suzuki Ignis
suzuki-ignis.tagline: Pocket crossover, raised ride for the gravel spur to Žanjic and Mirište
suzuki-ignis.lede: Tiny footprint plus genuine ride height. The pick for coves reached by dirt tracks, Žanjic, Mirište, the back road to Rose fishing village, without jumping to a full SUV.
suzuki-ignis.suitability: The fleet's quirky pick: higher ride height than a hatchback, smaller footprint than any SUV. Designed for Luštica's unpaved spurs to secluded beaches.
suzuki-ignis.regional: 180 mm ground clearance clears the deep ruts on the last 2 km of dirt track down to Žanjic beach, the 3.7 m length parks at the Sea Gate where a mid-size can't, and the four-cylinder engine is noticeably quieter than the 3-pot rivals.

citroen-c4.name: Citroen C4
citroen-c4.tagline: French mid-size with Advanced Comfort suspension, the softest ride in the fleet
citroen-c4.lede: 380-litre boot, PureTech 130, and Citroën's Advanced Comfort hydraulic bump stops that iron out the patched bitumen on the old coast road. The gentlest mid-size for long drives.
citroen-c4.suitability: The mid-size pick for renters who prioritise comfort over performance, perfect for a week mixing the Herceg Novi base with day-trips out to Budva, Stari Bar, or the north.
citroen-c4.regional: Progressive hydraulic bump stops smooth the broken stretch between Risan and Perast, the 380-litre boot fits four cases plus beach gear, and the 130 hp is plenty for overtaking slow tourist traffic on the E80 to Budva.

bestFor.2: Four adults on a slow tour

citroen-c3.name: Citroen C3
citroen-c3.tagline: Softest small hatch for the broken Herceg Novi–Risan stretch and back-road detours
citroen-c3.lede: Advanced Comfort dampers iron out the patched tarmac on the old bay road, the gentlest small car to park at the Herceg Novi bastions overnight.
citroen-c3.suitability: The comfort-first pick for a week in Herceg Novi with daily slow-paced detours, Risan's Roman mosaics, the Gornja Lastva hamlet above Tivat, the loop back through nearby villages.
citroen-c3.regional: The progressive hydraulic bump stops make the potholed stretch from Risan to Perast feel a size-class quieter, the low-stress 83 hp motor suits the 50 km/h bay-road limits, and the 4-metre length slots into any Old Town bastion bay.
citroen-c3.inlineAlt: Back road through Gornja Lastva above Tivat
citroen-c3.inlineCaption: The Gornja Lastva spur above the bay, the C3's Advanced Comfort turns the patched bitumen into distant thumps.

kia-stonic.name: Kia Stonic
kia-stonic.tagline: Raised-ride crossover for the gravel approach to Njeguši and Lovćen
kia-stonic.lede: A slightly higher-riding alternative to a hatchback, takes the rutted spur roads around Njeguši and the Lovćen back approaches without scraping.
kia-stonic.suitability: Renters who want crossover sight-lines and a bit of ground clearance for the side-roads around Herceg Novi's upper bay and the Lovćen approach, without paying full-SUV money.
kia-stonic.regional: Higher seating position means easier viewing over region parapet walls, the 182 mm ride height clears the stone ruts on the old Njeguši ham-smoker lane, and the 352-litre boot handles a family's Durmitor hiking gear without folding seats.
kia-stonic.inlineAlt: Rutted spur road up to Njeguši above Kotor
kia-stonic.inlineCaption: The Njeguši approach above the bay, a little extra ground clearance keeps the Stonic composed where a Clio scrapes.

vw-golf.name: VW Golf
vw-golf.tagline: DSG diesel all-rounder, calm cruising for the E80 and multi-day loops
vw-golf.lede: The rational default for a renter mixing a Herceg Novi base with long inland drives, refined, quick enough, and economical on the motorway.
vw-golf.suitability: The default mid-size for renters who want one car that will do the Herceg Novi Old Town perimeter on day one and the Smokovac-Mateševo motorway on day four without complaint.
vw-golf.regional: DSG picks the right ratio on the Herceg Novi–Lovćen hairpins without any thinking, the 2.0 TDI holds 130 km/h on the motorway section up to Podgorica at 4.3 L/100 km, and the cabin quiet makes the 3-hour push to Žabljak genuinely relaxing.
vw-golf.inlineCaption: The Kotor–Cetinje climb in third gear, the DSG reads the hairpins, the cabin stays silent.

bestFor.2: Drivers planning the BiH or Croatia border run

fiat-500.name: Fiat 500
fiat-500.tagline: Postage-stamp city car for a short Herceg Novi stay, slots between delivery vans
fiat-500.lede: Tiny footprint, sunroof, hybrid-assisted 1.0, the pick for a two- or three-night Herceg Novi anchor when multi-day drives aren't on the schedule.
fiat-500.suitability: Two or three nights in Herceg Novi with short afternoon hops to Perast and Prčanj, the 500 parks where mid-size cars wave and drive past.
fiat-500.regional: The 3.57 m length is the deciding factor: it fits the stepped terrace parking on the nearby villages waterfront and the shoulder-wide gap outside the Sea Gate that a Clio can't. Underpowered for the Lovćen climb, so pair with cable-car transfers or keep within bay limits.
fiat-500.inlineAlt: Narrow Muo waterfront lane in Kotor
fiat-500.inlineCaption: The Muo waterfront past the ferry dock, the 500 slots into gaps the rest of the fleet drives past.

fleetShowcase.label: Know your car
fleetShowcase.title: Guides to every car in our Herceg Novi fleet
fleetShowcase.subtitle: Specs, fuel range, boot size and which car earns its keep on the Orjen road, the Debeli Brijeg queue and the Kamenari ferry ramp.

fleetIndex.title: Our Herceg Novi Rental Fleet
fleetIndex.subtitle: Seven cars picked for a multi-day Herceg Novi base, with day-trips out to Kotor, Dubrovnik, Orjen and across to Trebinje.
fleetIndex.seoDesc: Seven multi-day rental cars for Herceg Novi, mid-size wagons, diesel cruisers and a city Fiat. Specs, fuel range, and which car suits day-trips out of the outer bay.
fleetIndex.intro1: This fleet is built around the multi-day Herceg Novi renter, visitors who anchor themselves at an Igalo apartment or an Old Town guesthouse for a fortnight or longer, and who expect to be spending real time on the road between bay-based days. That brief is different from a cruise-day hire or a pure Tivat Airport pickup: it leans on boot space for a weekly shop at Voli, diesel torque for the Debeli Brijeg border run, and a seating position that keeps four adults comfortable on the drive up Orjen to Ledenice. The seven cars below are chosen accordingly.
fleetIndex.intro2: The mix is deliberately weighted toward mid-size. A Peugeot 308 with the 1.5 BlueHDi and the EAT auto, a Renault Megane diesel with the segment's biggest boot, a VW Golf with the DSG 2.0 TDI, these are the cars that turn a Herceg Novi → Kamenari ferry → Kotor → Lovćen → Skadar Lake loop from a gear-changing chore into a cruise. For shorter hops we keep a Renault Clio, a Citroen C3 with Advanced Comfort dampers, a Kia Stonic crossover for the rutted spur roads up to Ledenice and Podi, and a tiny Fiat 500 for when the stepped Old Town lanes are the deciding constraint. No one-trick supercars, no vans; every car here justifies a multi-day hire.
fleetIndex.intro3: A practical Herceg Novi note before you choose. Diesel remains the smarter spec for anyone planning even one cross-border run, Dubrovnik via Debeli Brijeg is 40 minutes, Trebinje via the Sutorina valley is 1 h 15, and Mostar is a full day each way on a good cruise. The 308 and Megane will cover all three on a single tank, the Golf is the most refined motorway car we have, and the petrol Clio will still do a Herceg Novi → Dubrovnik return on half a tank. If your week is bay-contained, Igalo, Đenovići, Bijela, Perast via the ferry, the Fiat 500 or the C3 saves a third on weekly fuel and parks in places the rest of the fleet can't. Pick on your itinerary, not the daily rate.
